name: Structural Organisation in Animals
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Structural Organisation in Animals

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Structural Organisation in Animals

questions.question: Where is ciliated epithelium found in the human body?
questions.question_raw: Where is ciliated epithelium found in the human body?
questions.explanation: The ciliated epithelium is mainly present in the inner surface of hollow organs like bronchioles and fallopian tubes.

questions.question: The epithelium found in the lining of the stomach and intestine is ________.
questions.question_raw: The epithelium found in the lining of the stomach and intestine is ________.
questions.explanation: The columnar epithelium is found in the lining of the stomach and intestine and help in secretion and absorption. It also provides a barrier against harmful microorganisms.

questions.question: The epithelium involved in diffusion is:
questions.question_raw: The epithelium involved in diffusion is:
questions.explanation: The squamous epithelium is made of a single thin layer of flattened cells with irregular boundaries. They are found in the walls of blood vessels and air sacs of the lungs and are involved in functions like forming a diffusion boundary.

questions.question: Collagen is:
questions.question_raw: Collagen is:
questions.explanation: In all connective tissues except blood, the cells secrete fibres of structural proteins called collagen or elastin.

questions.question: Fibres and fibroblasts are compactly packed in:
questions.question_raw: Fibres and fibroblasts are compactly packed in:
questions.explanation: Dense irregular connective tissue provides strength to body parts. Hence the cells and fibres are packed compactly.

questions.question: Tendons connect:
questions.question_raw: Tendons connect:
questions.explanation: Tendons are dense connective tissue which provides strength and attach muscles to bones.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not a connective tissue?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is not a connective tissue?
questions.explanation:
Blood, bone, lymph forms the connective tissue that connects the different parts of the body
Nerve cells are parts of neural system or nervous system that transmit information from one part of the body to others.

questions.question: The epithelium found in proximal convoluted tubule (PCT) of the nephron in the kidney is_______.
questions.question_raw: The epithelium found in proximal convoluted tubule (PCT) of the nephron in the kidney is_______.
questions.explanation: The cuboidal epithelium is composed of a single layer of cube-like cells found in ducts of glands and tubular parts of nephrons in kidneys and its main functions are secretion and absorption.

questions.question: What makes up more than one-half the volume of neural tissue in our body?
questions.question_raw: What makes up more than one-half the volume of neural tissue in our body?
questions.explanation: The neuroglial cells which constitute the rest of the neural system protect and support neurons. Neuroglia makes up more than one-half the volume of neural tissue in our body.

questions.question: Intercalated discs occur in:
questions.question_raw: Intercalated discs occur in:

options.label: B
options.text: At the junction of muscle and nerve cells

questions.explanation: In between cardiac muscle fibres, there are communication junctions which are made up of intercalated discs.

questions.question: Fibroblasts, macrophages, and mast cells are present in ______.
questions.question_raw: Fibroblasts, macrophages, and mast cells are present in ______.
questions.explanation: Apart from being connective tissue, Areolar tissue is also protective in nature. It has a large number of fibroblast cells that produce fibres. Mast cells, plasma cells, and macrophages help to protect against pathogens and aid in tissue repair.

questions.question: The fibre that can't be made to contract merely by thinking about it, is the:
questions.question_raw: The fibre that can't be made to contract merely by thinking about it, is the:
questions.explanation: Smooth muscles are ‘involuntary’ muscles as they can't be directly controlled. We usually are not able to make it contract merely by thinking about it as we can do with skeletal muscles. Involuntary muscles are under the control of medulla oblongata and part of the cerebrum. Smooth muscles are spindle-shaped or fusiform, uninucleated, and unstriated fibres. The wall of internal organs such as the blood vessels, stomach, and intestine contains this type of muscle tissue.

questions.question: Identify the figure given below:
questions.question_raw: Identify the figure given below:
questions.explanation: The figure is identified as salivary gland. It is multicellular and is lined with glandular epithelium cells. These types of cells are specialized in production and release of particular kind of digestive juices or sweat or oil. Salivary gland secretes alkaline fluid called saliva.

questions.question: To which one of the following categories does adipose tissue belong?
questions.question_raw: To which one of the following categories does adipose tissue belong?
questions.explanation: Adipose tissue is a type of connective tissue consisting mainly of adipose cells or adipocytes, specialized to synthesize and contain large globules of fat.

questions.question: The junctions which help to stop substances from leaking across a tissue is:
questions.question_raw: The junctions which help to stop substances from leaking across a tissue is:
questions.explanation: Tight junctions hold cells together. They are narrow belts that circle around the upper part of lateral surfaces of the adjacent epithelial cells to create a fusion point. They stop substances from leaking across a tissue as they prevent the passage of molecules and ions through space between adjacent cells.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Structural Organisation in Animals

questions.question: The most abundant type of animal tissue in the complex animals is:
questions.question_raw: The most abundant type of animal tissue in the complex animals is:
questions.explanation: The most abundant type of animal tissue connective tissue as it forms a variety of tissues such as blood, bone, cartilage, adipose, lymph, tendon, and ligament. These connective tissue types perform a wide range of functions in the animal body such as giving support, packing, repairing, storing of fat, connecting different organs, etc.

questions.question: The formation of erythrocytes in foetus takes place in:
questions.question_raw: The formation of erythrocytes in foetus takes place in:
questions.explanation: In the early fetus, erythropoiesis takes place in the mesodermal cells of the yolk sac. Later, the production of RBCs starts in the fetal liver and increases until the 2nd^ trimester. After this, the rate of synthesis begins to decline in the liver as erythropoiesis increases in the bone marrow. RBCs are still produced in the liver for approximately 1 week after birth.

questions.question: Study of tissues is called:
questions.question_raw: Study of tissues is called:
questions.explanation: Study of organisms at tissues level is called histology. It includes the study of tissue structure, origin, location and function.

questions.question: The epithelium covering in the tongue is:
questions.question_raw: The epithelium covering in the tongue is:
questions.explanation: The tongue is covered by non-keratinized squamous epithelial tissues having taste buds of different kinds in different regions of the tongue.

questions.question: Two animal cells are interconnected by:
questions.question_raw: Two animal cells are interconnected by:
questions.explanation: Two animal cells are interconnected by desmosomes, through which the exchange of materials takes place between two cells. It is formed from protein plaques in the cell membranes linked by filaments.

questions.question: Myelin sheath is derived from:
questions.question_raw: Myelin sheath is derived from:
questions.explanation: The outer layer of the axon of nerve cells is called the myelin sheath. Myelin sheath is derived from Schwann cells.

questions.question: Antibodies are formed by:
questions.question_raw: Antibodies are formed by:
questions.explanation: Antibodies are formed against any foreign body entering the body. It is formed by plasma cells, globulins that develop resistance against a particular type of disease.

questions.question: The cell lining the blood vessels are:
questions.question_raw: The cell lining the blood vessels are:
questions.explanation: The endothelium is a type of epithelium that lines the interior surface of blood vessels and lymphatic vessels, forming an interface between circulating blood or lymph in the lumen and the rest of the vessel wall. It is a thin layer of simple squamous called endothelial cells.

questions.question: Cardiac muscles are:
questions.question_raw: Cardiac muscles are:
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscle tissue is a contractile tissue present only in the heart. The cardiac muscle fibres are elongated, branched and uninucleate. They appear striated due to the presence of alternate light and dark band. Cardiac muscles are self-contracting and involuntary in nature. The contract in a rhythmic fashion and help the heart to pump the blood.

questions.question: Which of the following structure is Ectodermal in origin?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following structure is Ectodermal in origin?
questions.explanation: The ectoderm differentiates to form the nervous system (spine, peripheral nerves and brain), tooth enamel and the epidermis, the outer part of the integument. It also forms the lining of mouth, anus, nostrils, sweat glands, hair and nails.

questions.question: A normal adult man and woman have ________ WBC per cubic millimetre.
questions.question_raw: A normal adult man and woman have ________ WBC per cubic millimetre.
questions.explanation: A normal adult male and female have about 4.5 to 5 million of WBC per cubic millimetre of blood. The number of white blood cells increases during infection against pathogens.

questions.question: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are:
questions.question_raw: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are:
questions.explanation: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are neutrophils and monocytes. These cells try to engulf the microbes that enter the blood to cause disease.

questions.question: Nasal septum contains:
questions.question_raw: Nasal septum contains:
questions.explanation: The nasal septum of human beings consists of hyaline cartilage which is highly elastic having collagen. It contains no nerves or blood vessels and is pearly bluish in colour.

questions.question: Lymph contains:
questions.question_raw: Lymph contains:
questions.explanation: Lymph contains serum and WBC. Lymph is called extracellular fluid that helps in the transport of fat and protein. It contains WBC, so also help in protection against the infection.

questions.question: Intercalated discs occur:
questions.question_raw: Intercalated discs occur:

options.label: B
options.text: At the junction of muscle and nerve cells

questions.explanation: Intercalated discs are microscopic identifying features of cardiac muscles fibres. Individual heart muscle cells are connected by intercalated discs to work as a single functional organ.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Structural Organisation in Animals

questions.question: Basophils help the body defence by:
questions.question_raw: Basophils help the body defence by:
questions.explanation: Basophils appear in many specific kinds of inflammatory reactions, particularly those that cause allergic symptoms. Basophils contain anticoagulant heparin, which prevents blood from clotting too quickly. (Thrombosisis the formation of a blood clot inside a blood vessel, obstructing the flow of blood through the circulatory system.) They also contain the vasodilator histamine, which promotes blood flow to tissues.

questions.question: The life span of RBC and WBC are respectively:
questions.question_raw: The life span of RBC and WBC are respectively:
questions.explanation: Red blood cells (RBC) live for 120 days and white blood cells (WBC) live for 90 days. Afterwards, they are degraded in the spleen.

questions.question: Nissl granules in a nerve cell are now identified as_______.
questions.question_raw: Nissl granules in a nerve cell are now identified as_______.
questions.explanation: The Nissl granules in nerve cells are now identified as ribosomes after the discovery of an electron microscope that provides a highly magnified image for detailed study.

questions.question: Which one of the following is the correct pairing of a body part and muscle fibre that moves it?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is the correct pairing of a body part and muscle fibre that moves it?

options.label: B
options.text: Biceps of upper arm - smooth muscle fibres

questions.explanation: The iris contains involuntary smooth muscles. It encircles the pupil of the iris and helps to dilate the pupil when there is insufficient light for the normal function of the eye.

questions.question: Which of the following leucocytes transforms into macrophages?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following leucocytes transforms into macrophages?
questions.explanation: Monocytes are white blood cells or leucocytes that transform into macrophages to engulf the pathogen entering the body.

questions.question: Intestinal mucosa has microvili to:
questions.question_raw: Intestinal mucosa has microvili to:
questions.explanation: The innermost layer of the intestinal wall is called the mucosa. Mucosa contains microvilli to increase the surface area for the absorption of digested food to transport into a different part of the body.

questions.question: Which one is a heterocrine gland?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a heterocrine gland?
questions.explanation: The pancreas is called heterocrine gland because it acts as both endocrine and exocrine gland to secrete hormone as well as digestive enzymes. Testis and Ovary are also heterocrine glands.

questions.question: Blood plasma is pale yellow in colour due to:
questions.question_raw: Blood plasma is pale yellow in colour due to:
questions.explanation: Blood plasma is pale yellow in colour due to the presence of bilirubin pigment in it. Blood appears red due to the presence of haemoglobin in RBC.

questions.question: Muscle forming cells are called:
questions.question_raw: Muscle forming cells are called:
questions.explanation: The muscle forming cells are called myoblasts cells. Myoblast is a type of embryonic progenitor cell that differentiates to give rise to muscle cells.

questions.question: Which layer produces a new cell by mitosis?
questions.question_raw: Which layer produces a new cell by mitosis?
questions.explanation: Stratum Malpighi is the basal layer of skin epidermis having basal layer and generative layer that produces new cells by mitosis.

questions.question: A largest single mass of lymphatic tissue in the body is______.
questions.question_raw: A largest single mass of lymphatic tissue in the body is______.
questions.explanation: Spleen is the largest single mass of lymphatic tissue in the body. Blood formation in the foetus and newly born child takes place in spleen.

questions.question: Both WBC and RBC are formed in the:
questions.question_raw: Both WBC and RBC are formed in the:
questions.explanation: In adults, both WBC and RBC are formed in the bone marrow that is located in long bones such as thighs.

questions.question: Mature erythrocytes cannot utilise glucose because they lack_______.
questions.question_raw: Mature erythrocytes cannot utilise glucose because they lack_______.
questions.explanation: Mature erythrocytes or red blood corpuscles cannot utilize glucose because these cells lack mitochondria as well as the nucleus. Though glycolysis or glucose breakdown occurs in the cytoplasm, the final breakdown of pyruvate takes place inside mitochondria. Also, the electron transport chain is located inside mitochondria. Hence, the presence of mitochondria is essential for a cell to utilize glucose and release energy.

questions.question: The matrix of bone and cartilage can be distinguished by the presence of ________.
questions.question_raw: The matrix of bone and cartilage can be distinguished by the presence of ________.
questions.explanation: The matrix of bone and cartilage differ by the presence of a Haversian canal that makes bone hard and stiff. On the other hand, cartilage is flexible.

questions.question: The ciliated columnar epithelium cells in human are known to occur in:
questions.question_raw: The ciliated columnar epithelium cells in human are known to occur in:
questions.explanation: Ciliated columnar epithelium cells in humans occur in bronchioles and fallopian tube. They contain small hair-like cilia on the free surface. This helps to move egg into the fallopian tube. In bronchioles, it helps to move mucus or dust particles.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Structural Organisation in Animals

questions.question: Whales can live in cold water as they have thick coat of_________.
questions.question_raw: Whales can live in cold water as they have thick coat of_________.
questions.explanation: Whales can live in cold water as they have a thick coat of blubber. Blubber help in providing insulating coat beneath the skin that prevents loss of heat from the body.

questions.question: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of________.
questions.question_raw: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of________.
questions.explanation: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of neurilemma. They are the outermost nucleated cytoplasmic layer of Schwann cells that surrounds the axon of the neuron.

questions.question: Platelets are formed from the________.
questions.question_raw: Platelets are formed from the________.
questions.explanation: Platelets are a little piece of blood cells that help wound healing and prevent bleeding by forming a blood clot. It is formed from the megakaryocytes.

questions.question: Certain neutrophils in female mammals possess a small spherical lobe attached to their nucleus by a stalk. This lobe is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Certain neutrophils in female mammals possess a small spherical lobe attached to their nucleus by a stalk. This lobe is called ________.
questions.explanation: A Barr body appears as a small drumstick-like projection on one of the lobes of some neutrophils in females. A Barr body is the inactive X chromosome in a female somatic cell.

questions.question: The type of connective tissue that is associated with the umbilical cord is:
questions.question_raw: The type of connective tissue that is associated with the umbilical cord is:
questions.explanation: Jelly like connective tissue is associated with the umbilical cord. It is a flexible cord-like structure containing blood vessels and attaching a human fetus to the placenta during gestation.

questions.question: The haemoglobin content per 100 ml of blood of a normal healthy human adult is:
questions.question_raw: The haemoglobin content per 100 ml of blood of a normal healthy human adult is:
questions.explanation: The haemoglobin content per 100 ml of blood of a normal healthy human adult is in the range of 12-16 g. Haemoglobin acts as a respiratory carrier. It is responsible for picking up oxygen from the lungs and transporting it to all parts of the body through blood. Maintaining a healthy level of haemoglobin in blood is essential for sufficient supply of oxygen to cells or tissues. Level of haemoglobin less than 12g/100ml leads to anaemia and weakness.

questions.question: The type of muscles present in our:
questions.question_raw: The type of muscles present in our:

options.label: A
options.text: the heart is involuntary and unstriated smooth muscle.

options.label: B
options.text: the upper arm is smooth muscle and fusiform in shape.

options.label: C
options.text: the intestine is striated and involuntary.

options.label: D
options.text: the thigh is striated and voluntary.

correct_option_texts: the thigh is striated and voluntary.
questions.explanation: The muscle present in our thigh is striated and voluntary muscles. They are attached to the bone for movement and works according to our will.

questions.question: Choose the odd pair out in the following:
questions.question_raw: Choose the odd pair out in the following:
questions.explanation: Epithelium tissues contain keratin, muscles fibres contain actin and areolar connective tissue contain collagen but melanin is absent in neuron.

questions.question: A close approximation of an axon of one neuron and a dendrite of the next is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A close approximation of an axon of one neuron and a dendrite of the next is called ________.
questions.explanation: The close connection between an axon and dendrite of the next neuron is called a synapse. Nerve impulses pass through it in the form of neurotransmitters due to the presence of synaptic fluid present in it.

questions.question: The muscle which is immune to fatigue are:
questions.question_raw: The muscle which is immune to fatigue are:
questions.explanation: The muscles which are immune to fatigue are cardiac muscles present exclusively in the heart. They work from birth till death without getting fatigued.

questions.question: The mammary gland is an example of:
questions.question_raw: The mammary gland is an example of:
questions.explanation: Apocrine glands are a kind of exocrine gland found in the skin, breast, eyelids, and ears. Mammary glands are present in the breast that release milk in mammals.

questions.question: In earthworm, two pairs of testes are present in:
questions.question_raw: In earthworm, two pairs of testes are present in:
questions.explanation: There are two pairs of testes present in the 10th and 11th segments. Their vasa deferentia run-up to the 18th segment where they join the prostatic duct.

questions.question: How many types of nephridia are seen in earthworm based on their location?
questions.question_raw: How many types of nephridia are seen in earthworm based on their location?
questions.explanation:
The excretory organs occur as segmentally arranged coiled tubules called nephridia.
They are of three types:
 septal nephridia
 integumentary nephridia
 pharyngeal nephridia.

questions.question: The pharyngeal nephridia of earthworm occur in segments:
questions.question_raw: The pharyngeal nephridia of earthworm occur in segments:
questions.explanation: The excretory system of earthworm contains a pair of nephridia in every segment, except for the first three and the last ones. Three types of nephridia are found in earthworm: integumentary, septal, and pharyngeal. The pharyngeal nephridia are present as three paired tufts in the 4th, 5th, and 6th segments.

questions.question: Earthworms are known as Friends of farmers because:
questions.question_raw: Earthworms are known as Friends of farmers because:

options.label: B
options.text: They are used as meal for farmers

questions.explanation: Earthworms are known as ‘friends of farmers’ because they make burrows in the soil and make it porous which helps in respiration and penetration of the developing plant roots.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Structural Organisation in Animals

questions.question: In Pheretima, ovaries are present in:
questions.question_raw: In Pheretima, ovaries are present in:
questions.explanation: In Pheretima a pair of whitish, small, lobulated ovaries are present on either side of the ventral nerve cord in 13th segments.

questions.question: In earthworm, the stomach is present in:
questions.question_raw: In earthworm, the stomach is present in:
questions.explanation: The stomach of an earthworm is a long cylindrical structure and extends from 9-14 segments.

questions.question:
Which statement is wrong with respect to an earthworm?
 Muscular layer in the body wall of an earthworm is made up of circular muscles.
 Setae help in the locomotion.
 A single male genital pore is present.
 Typhlosole is the part of the intestine.
questions.question_raw:
Which statement is wrong with respect to an earthworm?

  1. Muscular layer in the body wall of an earthworm is made up of circular muscles.
  2. Setae help in the locomotion.
  3. A single male genital pore is present.
  4. Typhlosole is the part of the intestine.
questions.explanation: The body wall of the earthworm is covered externally by a thin non-cellular cuticle below which is the epidermis, two muscle layers namely, circular and longitudinal, and an innermost coelomic epithelium.

questions.question: The clitellum in earthworm occur in:
questions.question_raw: The clitellum in earthworm occur in:
questions.explanation: In a mature worm, segments 14-16 are covered by a prominent dark band of glandular tissue called the clitellum.

questions.question: Which of the following structure is Ectodermal in origin in the mouthparts of cockroach shown in the figure below?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following structure is Ectodermal in origin in the mouthparts of cockroach shown in the figure below?
questions.explanation: The maxilla is a paired structure present in the mouth part of cockroaches. It is derived from ectoderm and is used to hold and manipulate food.

questions.question: How many pairs of spiracles are found in cockroach?
questions.question_raw: How many pairs of spiracles are found in cockroach?
questions.explanation: The respiratory system consists of a network of the trachea. This network opens through 10 pairs of small holes called spiracles present on the lateral side of the body.

questions.question: Identify ‘A’ in the figure given below.
questions.question_raw: Identify ‘A’ in the figure given below.
questions.explanation: The label ‘A’ is ocellus. It is a type of photoreceptor found in some animals like cockroaches.

questions.question: In male cockroaches, genital pouch lies at the hind end of the abdomen bounded by:
questions.question_raw: In male cockroaches, genital pouch lies at the hind end of the abdomen bounded by:

options.label: A
options.text: Dorsally by 9th and 10th terga and ventrally by the 9th sternum

options.label: B
options.text: Dorsally by 8th and 9th terga and ventrally by the 9th sternum

options.label: C
options.text: Dorsally by 8th and 9th terga and ventrally by the 8th sternum

options.label: D
options.text: Dorsally by 9th and 10th terga and ventrally by the 10th sternum

correct_option_texts: Dorsally by 9th and 10th terga and ventrally by the 9th sternum
questions.explanation: In males, genital pouch or chamber lies at the hind end of abdomen bounded dorsally by 9th and 10th terga and ventrally by the 9th sternum. It contains dorsal anus, ventral male genital pore, and gonapophysis.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is true for cockroach?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following statements is true for cockroach?

options.label: A
options.text: The larval stage is called caterpillar

options.label: B
options.text: The number of ovarioles in each ovary are ten

options.label: D
options.text: Anal styles are absent in females

correct_option_texts: Anal styles are absent in females
questions.explanation: Male cockroaches bear a pair of short, thread-like anal styles in the 9th segment which is absent in females.

questions.question: The number of eggs contained in an ootheca of cockroach is:
questions.question_raw: The number of eggs contained in an ootheca of cockroach is:
questions.explanation: On average, females produce 9-10 oothecae. Each ootheca contains 14-16 eggs.

questions.question: The number of ganglia in the nervous system of cockroach is:
questions.question_raw: The number of ganglia in the nervous system of cockroach is:

options.label: A
options.text: 6 in thorax and 3 in the abdomen

options.label: B
options.text: 4 in thorax and 6 in the abdomen

options.label: C
options.text: 3 in thorax and 6 in the abdomen

options.label: D
options.text: 4 in thorax and 3 in the abdomen

correct_option_texts: 3 in thorax and 6 in the abdomen
questions.explanation: The nervous system of cockroach consists of a series of fused, segmentally arranged ganglia. They are joined by paired longitudinal connectives on the ventral side. Three ganglia lie in the thorax, and six ganglia are found in the abdomen.

questions.question: What type of metamorphosis occurs in cockroach?
questions.question_raw: What type of metamorphosis occurs in cockroach?
questions.explanation: The development of cockroach is paurometabolous. This means a gradual development from the nymphal stage to the adult stage. Nymph represents a miniature adult and lives in the same habitat.

questions.question: In cockroach, a mushroom-shaped gland is present in:
questions.question_raw: In cockroach, a mushroom-shaped gland is present in:
questions.explanation: A characteristic mushroom-shaped gland is present in the 6th-7th abdominal segments of the cockroach. This gland functions as an accessory reproductive gland.

questions.question:
Match the following and choose the correct answer with reference to cockroach:
 Column A
 Column B
 (i) Bundles of sperm
 (a) Ovarioles
 (ii) External genitalia
 (b) Gonopore
 (iii) Chain of developing ova
 (c) Phallomere
 (iv) Opening of ejaculatory duct
 (d) Spermatophore
questions.question_raw:
Match the following and choose the correct answer with reference to cockroach:

Column A Column B
(i) Bundles of sperm (a) Ovarioles
(ii) External genitalia (b) Gonopore
(iii) Chain of developing ova (c) Phallomere
(iv) Opening of ejaculatory duct (d) Spermatophore
questions.explanation:
The sperms are stored in the seminal vesicles and are glued together in the form of bundles called spermatophores which are discharged during copulation.
 The external genitalia is represented by chitinous asymmetrical structures surrounding the male gonopore is called male gonapophysis or phallomere.
 Each ovary is formed of a group of eight ovarian tubules or ovarioles, containing a chain of developing ova. 
 The ejaculatory duct opens into a male gonopore situated ventral to the anus.

questions.question: The pericardial space in cockroach is regularly altered by the muscles:
questions.question_raw: The pericardial space in cockroach is regularly altered by the muscles:
questions.explanation: Cockroaches have an open circulatory system. Their pericardial space is regularly altered by alary muscles.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Structural Organisation in Animals

questions.question: The mouth part of cockroach which is compared to the tongue is_______.
questions.question_raw: The mouth part of cockroach which is compared to the tongue is_______.
questions.explanation: A median flexible lobe called a hypopharynx act as the tongue. It lies within the cavity enclosed by the mouthparts.

questions.question: How many times do nymphs moult to reach the adult form of a cockroach?
questions.question_raw: How many times do nymphs moult to reach the adult form of a cockroach?
questions.explanation: When the ootheca hatches, a tiny nymph comes out. It grows by undergoing molting about 13 times to reach the adult form.

questions.question: Blood consists of:
questions.question_raw: Blood consists of:
questions.explanation: Blood is a fluid connective tissue containing plasma. It contains red blood cells (RBC), white blood cells (WBC), and platelets suspended in the plasma.

questions.question: In frog, during aestivation and hibernation gaseous exchange takes place through
questions.question_raw: In frog, during aestivation and hibernation gaseous exchange takes place through
questions.explanation: As the frog becomes inactive during aestivation and hibernation, gaseous exchange does not take place through lungs. It takes place through skin.

questions.question: The lymph in frog lacks:
questions.question_raw: The lymph in frog lacks:
questions.explanation: The lymph is different from the blood. It is known as tissue fluid. Lymph is yellowish in colour as it lacks RBCs and few proteins.

questions.question: The winter sleep in frog is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The winter sleep in frog is called ________.
questions.explanation: During extreme heat and cold periods, the frog takes shelter in deep burrows to protect them from extreme environmental conditions. This is called as summer sleep (aestivation) and winter sleep (hibernation).

questions.question:
The correct order of the digestive system of the frog is:
 Mouth, buccal cavity, oesophagus, stomach, intestine, rectum, cloaca, cloacal aperture
 Mouth, oesophagus, buccal cavity, stomach, intestine, rectum, cloacal aperture, cloaca
 Mouth, buccal cavity, oesophagus, intestine, stomach, rectum, cloacal aperture, cloaca
 Mouth, oesophagus, buccal cavity, stomach, rectum, intestine, cloaca, cloacal aperture, cloaca
questions.question_raw:
The correct order of the digestive system of the frog is:

  1. Mouth, buccal cavity, oesophagus, stomach, intestine, rectum, cloaca, cloacal aperture
  2. Mouth, oesophagus, buccal cavity, stomach, intestine, rectum, cloacal aperture, cloaca
  3. Mouth, buccal cavity, oesophagus, intestine, stomach, rectum, cloacal aperture, cloaca
  4. Mouth, oesophagus, buccal cavity, stomach, rectum, intestine, cloaca, cloacal aperture, cloaca
questions.explanation: The digestive system of a frog begins from the mouth which opens into the buccal cavity that leads to the esophagus through the pharynx. The esophagus is a short tube that opens into the stomach which in turn continues as the intestine and then rectum. The rectum finally opens outside by the cloaca through the cloacal aperture.

questions.question: The special venous connection between liver and intestine as well as the kidney and lower parts of the body present in frogs is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The special venous connection between liver and intestine as well as the kidney and lower parts of the body present in frogs is called ________.
questions.explanation: The renal portal system forms a special blood circulation system between liver and intestine. It also forms a special connection between the kidney and lower parts of the body present in frogs.

questions.question: The number of nymphs produced from single ootheca of the frog is _______.
questions.question_raw: The number of nymphs produced from single ootheca of the frog is _______.
questions.explanation: Young ones of frogs are called nymphs. When the ootheca ruptures, 16 nymphs come out.

questions.question:
Match the following with reference to the frog:
 Column A
 Column B
 (i) Medulla oblongata
 (a) Sensory papillae
 (ii) Smell
 (b) PNS
 (iii) Touch
 (c) Foramen magnum
 (iv) Cranial nerves
 (d) Nasal epithelium
questions.question_raw:
Match the following with reference to the frog:

Column A Column B
(i) Medulla oblongata (a) Sensory papillae
(ii) Smell (b) PNS
(iii) Touch (c) Foramen magnum
(iv) Cranial nerves (d) Nasal epithelium
questions.explanation:
The medulla oblongata passes out through the foramen magnum and continues into the spinal cord, which is enclosed in the vertebral column.
 Frog has different types of sense organs. This includes organs of touch (sensory papillae) and smell (nasal epithelium).
 The nervous system is organized into a central nervous system including the brain and spinal cord and peripheral nervous system (PNS) including cranial and spinal nerves.

questions.question: Blood plasma contains ________ % water.
questions.question_raw: Blood plasma contains ________ % water.
questions.explanation: Blood consists of blood plasma formed cells ( RBC, WBC, Platelets) along with sugar, proteins, etc. Blood plasma contains about 90% water with a number of salts and few circulating hormones.

questions.question: Myelin sheath is mainly made of______.
questions.question_raw: Myelin sheath is mainly made of______.
questions.explanation: The myelin sheath is the outer layer of the axon of nerve cells. It is made up of phospholipids. It is mainly protective in nature, acts as an insulator, and helps in the transmission of impulses.

questions.question: The formation of erythrocytes in foetus takes place in:
questions.question_raw: The formation of erythrocytes in foetus takes place in:
questions.explanation: In the early fetus, erythropoiesis takes place in the mesodermal cells of the yolk sac. Later, the production of RBCs starts in the fetal liver and increases until the 2nd^ trimester. After this, the rate of synthesis begins to decline in the liver as erythropoiesis increases in the bone marrow. RBCs are still produced in the liver for approximately 1 week after birth.

questions.question: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of________.
questions.question_raw: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of________.
questions.explanation: Nerve fibres in the CNS can regenerate after injury due to the presence of neurilemma. They are the outermost nucleated cytoplasmic layer of Schwann cells that surrounds the axon of the neuron.

questions.question: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are:
questions.question_raw: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are:
questions.explanation: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are neutrophils and monocytes. These cells try to engulf the microbes that enter the blood to cause disease.
